name: deep_research_assistant
description: Research a given topic on the open web, synthesize a structured multi-section report, and save it to a dated markdown file. Unattended; never asks the user anything.

variables.description: Desired depth/length of the report, e.g. 'Standard - 3-5 pages'.
Raw research collected on the topic: findings, key statistics, recent developments, stakeholder perspectives, and source URLs.
The final structured report synthesized from research_findings, written to the dated results file.

settings.action: Using the `topic` and `research_depth` variables, search the web for comprehensive, current information on the topic. Collect findings, key statistics, recent developments, different stakeholder perspectives, and source URLs into the `research_findings` slot.

Using `research_findings`, build a structured report into the `research_report` slot with these sections: Executive Summary (3-5 key findings, bottom line conclusion, recommended action), Background & Context, Current State, Key Findings (with supporting evidence), Different Perspectives (areas of consensus/debate), Implications (risks/opportunities), Recommended Actions (prioritized next steps), Sources (with reliability rating and any gaps noted), and Questions for Further Research.

Write the contents of `research_report` to a markdown file named `deep_research_assistant_<timestamp>.md` in the current working directory, where <timestamp> is the current date/time. Do not ask any questions; this is an unattended run.
